title: Register a domestic company
description: Set up a Barbadian company under the Companies Act, Cap. 308.
summary: If you want to set up a company that operates in Barbados, you must register it with the Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO). This is the standard route for any business that is not an International Business Company or a non-profit.

Registration is done under the Companies Act, Cap. 308. A Barbadian attorney-at-law has to sign the declaration form on your behalf – the law requires it. Most people work with an attorney-at-law throughout the process, but you can also use a registered corporate services provider.

Before you start, decide on a name. Reserve the name with CAIPO first, because if it is already in use, you will need to choose another. You will also need to decide on the directors, the shareholders and the share structure.
Once incorporated, the company gets a certificate of incorporation and a unique company number. You can then open a bank account in the company’s name, register for taxes and start trading.
who_can_apply: anyone over 18 who wants to set up a company in Barbados
a Barbadian attorney-at-law acting on behalf of the applicant

items: the name of the company (and one or two alternatives in case it is taken)
the type of business the company will do
the address of the registered office
the names, addresses and dates of birth of every director
the names and addresses of every shareholder
the share structure of the company

items.name: Notice of address of registered office (Form 4)
Declaration form signed by your attorney-at-law
Proof of payment of the registration fee

how_long: A decision usually takes 5 to 10 working days.

options.heading: Through an attorney-at-law
content: A Barbadian attorney-at-law will prepare the documents and file them with the Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office.

contact.agency: Corporate Affairs and Intellectual Property Office (CAIPO)
contact.address:
Baobab Tower, Ground Floor
Warrens
St. Michael

assumptions: Name reservation fee of $25 BBD and incorporation fee of $750 BBD are illustrative. Actual fees are set by CAIPO and may have changed.
‘A decision usually takes 5 to 10 working days’ is an estimate.
‘A Barbadian attorney-at-law has to sign the declaration form on your behalf – the law requires it’ is the long-standing rule. The form itself just refers to ‘Attorney-at-Law’.
List of supporting documents (Form 4, Form 9, notice of directors, notice of registered office) is based on standard CAIPO practice.
